xanesnet.models.gemnet package¶

Public API for the GemNet directional graph neural network model.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.GemNet(model_type, num_spherical, num_radial, num_blocks, emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_quad, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_sbf, emb_size_bil_quad, emb_size_bil_trip, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_concat, num_atom, triplets_only, num_targets, cutoff, int_cutoff, envelope_exponent, output_init, activation, scale_file, num_elements)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Universal directional graph neural network (GemNet).

Reference: GemNet: Universal Directional Graph Neural Networks for Molecules.

Implementation based on TUM-DAML/gemnet_pytorch.

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type string (passed to base class).

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical harmonics / basis functions.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • num_blocks (int) – Number of interaction blocks.

  • emb_size_atom (int) – Embedding size for atoms.

  • emb_size_edge (int) – Embedding size for edges.

  • emb_size_trip (int) – Down-projected embedding size in the triplet interaction block.

  • emb_size_quad (int) – Down-projected embedding size in the quadruplet interaction block.

  • emb_size_rbf (int) – Embedding size of the radial basis projection.

  • emb_size_cbf (int) – Embedding size of the circular basis projection.

  • emb_size_sbf (int) – Embedding size of the spherical basis projection.

  • emb_size_bil_quad (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer in the quadruplet block.

  • emb_size_bil_trip (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer in the triplet block.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual blocks before each skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual blocks after each skip connection.

  • num_concat (int) – Number of residual blocks after the atom-edge concatenation.

  • num_atom (int) – Number of residual blocks in the atom update block.

  • triplets_only (bool) – If True, use GemNet-T/dT (triplets only, no quadruplets).

  • num_targets (int) – Output dimension per atom.

  • cutoff (float) – Main graph edge cutoff in A.

  • int_cutoff (float) – Interaction graph edge cutoff in A (quadruplet variant only).

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent p for the polynomial cutoff envelope.

  • output_init (str) – Weight initialization for the final output layer ("HeOrthogonal" or "zeros").

  • activation (str) – Activation function name ("swish" / "silu").

  • scale_file (str | None) – Path to a JSON file with pre-fitted variance scale factors, or None to use the default value of 1.0 for all factors.

  • num_elements (int) – Number of distinct element types in the embedding table.

__init__(model_type, num_spherical, num_radial, num_blocks, emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_quad, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_sbf, emb_size_bil_quad, emb_size_bil_trip, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_concat, num_atom, triplets_only, num_targets, cutoff, int_cutoff, envelope_exponent, output_init, activation, scale_file, num_elements)[source]¶

Initialize GemNet.

static calculate_neighbor_angles(R_ac, R_ab)[source]¶

Compute the angle between two bond vectors at a shared atom.

Parameters:

  • R_ac (Tensor) – Bond vectors from atom a to atom c, shape (N, 3).

  • R_ab (Tensor) – Bond vectors from atom a to atom b, shape (N, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Angles in radians, shape (N,).

static vector_rejection(R_ab, P_n)[source]¶

Project R_ab onto the plane whose normal is P_n.

Parameters:

  • R_ab (Tensor) – Vectors to project, shape (N, 3).

  • P_n (Tensor) – Normal vectors of the projection plane, shape (N, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Projected vectors orthogonal to P_n, shape (N, 3).

static calculate_angles3(edge_vec, id3_reduce_ca, id3_expand_ba)[source]¶

Compute triplet angles for message passing.

Uses pre-computed PBC-correct edge vectors to avoid NaN for periodic self-image edges where pos[id_a] == pos[id_c].

Parameters:

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – PBC-aware edge vectors (id_c -> id_a), shape (nEdges, 3).

  • id3_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Edge index of c -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

  • id3_expand_ba (Tensor) – Edge index of b -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Triplet angles at atom a, shape (nTriplets,).

static calculate_angles(edge_vec, int_edge_vec, id4_expand_abd, id4_reduce_cab, id4_expand_intm_db, id4_reduce_intm_ca, id4_expand_intm_ab, id4_reduce_intm_ab)[source]¶

Compute the three angles required for quadruplet-based message passing.

Uses PBC-aware pre-computed edge vectors to avoid NaN on periodic self-image edges. The three returned angles correspond to:

  • angle_cab: angle at atom a between bonds c <- a -> b.

  • angle_abd: angle at atom b between bonds a <- b -> d.

  • angle_cabd: dihedral-like angle between the projected planes c <- a-b -> d.

Parameters:

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – Main-graph edge vectors (id_c -> id_a), shape (nEdges, 3).

  • int_edge_vec (Tensor) – Interaction-graph edge vectors (id4_int_b -> id4_int_a), shape (nIntEdges, 3).

  • id4_expand_abd (Tensor) – Quadruplet index mapping to the a-b-d triplet.

  • id4_reduce_cab (Tensor) – Quadruplet index mapping to the c-a-b triplet.

  • id4_expand_intm_db (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet index for the d -> b edge.

  • id4_reduce_intm_ca (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet index for the c -> a edge.

  • id4_expand_intm_ab (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet index for the a -> b edge.

  • id4_reduce_intm_ab (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet index for the a -> b edge (reduce side).

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

Returns:

Tuple (angle_cab, angle_abd, angle_cabd), each of shape (nQuadruplets,).

forward(z, edge_vec, edge_weight, id_a, id_c, id_swap, id3_expand_ba, id3_reduce_ca, Kidx3, int_edge_vec=None, int_edge_weight=None, Kidx4=None, id4_reduce_ca=None, id4_reduce_cab=None, id4_expand_abd=None, id4_reduce_intm_ca=None, id4_expand_intm_db=None, id4_reduce_intm_ab=None, id4_expand_intm_ab=None)[source]¶

Run the GemNet forward pass and return per-atom target predictions.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (nAtoms,).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – PBC-aware main-graph edge vectors (id_c -> id_a), shape (nEdges, 3).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Main-graph edge lengths (distances) in A, shape (nEdges,).

  • id_a (Tensor) – Target atom index for each edge (c -> a), shape (nEdges,).

  • id_c (Tensor) – Source atom index for each edge (c -> a), shape (nEdges,).

  • id_swap (Tensor) – Index mapping each edge to its reverse, shape (nEdges,).

  • id3_expand_ba (Tensor) – Edge index of b -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

  • id3_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Edge index of c -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

  • Kidx3 (Tensor) – Neighbor index within the sparse dense matrix for triplets, shape (nTriplets,).

  • int_edge_vec (Tensor | None) – Interaction-graph edge vectors, shape (nIntEdges, 3). Required when triplets_only=False.

  • int_edge_weight (Tensor | None) – Interaction-graph edge lengths in A, shape (nIntEdges,). Required when triplets_only=False.

  • Kidx4 (Tensor | None) – Neighbor index for the quadruplet sparse dense matrix. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_reduce_ca (Tensor | None) – Quadruplet edge index c -> a. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_reduce_cab (Tensor | None) – Quadruplet reduce index for c-a-b. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_expand_abd (Tensor | None) – Quadruplet expand index for a-b-d. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_reduce_intm_ca (Tensor | None) – Intermediate-triplet index c -> a. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_expand_intm_db (Tensor | None) – Intermediate-triplet index d -> b. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_reduce_intm_ab (Tensor | None) – Intermediate-triplet index a -> b (reduce side). Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_expand_intm_ab (Tensor | None) – Intermediate-triplet index a -> b (expand side). Required when triplets_only=False.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom target predictions of shape (nAtoms, num_targets).

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize GemNet weights using its built-in He-orthogonal scheme.

GemNet uses its own weight initialization (He-orthogonal + variance- preserving scale factors), so the weights_init and bias_init arguments required by the base-class interface are intentionally ignored. A warning is emitted when called.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Ignored. Present for base-class interface compatibility.

  • bias_init (str) – Ignored. Present for base-class interface compatibility.

  • **kwargs – Additional keyword arguments (ignored).

Return type:

None

property signature: Config¶

Return the model signature as a Config.

Note

scale_file is always set to None in the signature. The fitted scale-factor values are carried by the model state_dict as non-trainable parameters, making saved checkpoints fully portable.

Submodules¶

xanesnet.models.gemnet.gemnet module¶

GemNet universal directional graph neural network for molecular property prediction.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.gemnet.GemNet(model_type, num_spherical, num_radial, num_blocks, emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_quad, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_sbf, emb_size_bil_quad, emb_size_bil_trip, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_concat, num_atom, triplets_only, num_targets, cutoff, int_cutoff, envelope_exponent, output_init, activation, scale_file, num_elements)[source]¶

Bases: Model

Universal directional graph neural network (GemNet).

Reference: GemNet: Universal Directional Graph Neural Networks for Molecules.

Implementation based on TUM-DAML/gemnet_pytorch.

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type string (passed to base class).

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical harmonics / basis functions.

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • num_blocks (int) – Number of interaction blocks.

  • emb_size_atom (int) – Embedding size for atoms.

  • emb_size_edge (int) – Embedding size for edges.

  • emb_size_trip (int) – Down-projected embedding size in the triplet interaction block.

  • emb_size_quad (int) – Down-projected embedding size in the quadruplet interaction block.

  • emb_size_rbf (int) – Embedding size of the radial basis projection.

  • emb_size_cbf (int) – Embedding size of the circular basis projection.

  • emb_size_sbf (int) – Embedding size of the spherical basis projection.

  • emb_size_bil_quad (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer in the quadruplet block.

  • emb_size_bil_trip (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer in the triplet block.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual blocks before each skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual blocks after each skip connection.

  • num_concat (int) – Number of residual blocks after the atom-edge concatenation.

  • num_atom (int) – Number of residual blocks in the atom update block.

  • triplets_only (bool) – If True, use GemNet-T/dT (triplets only, no quadruplets).

  • num_targets (int) – Output dimension per atom.

  • cutoff (float) – Main graph edge cutoff in A.

  • int_cutoff (float) – Interaction graph edge cutoff in A (quadruplet variant only).

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent p for the polynomial cutoff envelope.

  • output_init (str) – Weight initialization for the final output layer ("HeOrthogonal" or "zeros").

  • activation (str) – Activation function name ("swish" / "silu").

  • scale_file (str | None) – Path to a JSON file with pre-fitted variance scale factors, or None to use the default value of 1.0 for all factors.

  • num_elements (int) – Number of distinct element types in the embedding table.

__init__(model_type, num_spherical, num_radial, num_blocks, emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_quad, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_sbf, emb_size_bil_quad, emb_size_bil_trip, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_concat, num_atom, triplets_only, num_targets, cutoff, int_cutoff, envelope_exponent, output_init, activation, scale_file, num_elements)[source]¶

Initialize GemNet.

static calculate_neighbor_angles(R_ac, R_ab)[source]¶

Compute the angle between two bond vectors at a shared atom.

Parameters:

  • R_ac (Tensor) – Bond vectors from atom a to atom c, shape (N, 3).

  • R_ab (Tensor) – Bond vectors from atom a to atom b, shape (N, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Angles in radians, shape (N,).

static vector_rejection(R_ab, P_n)[source]¶

Project R_ab onto the plane whose normal is P_n.

Parameters:

  • R_ab (Tensor) – Vectors to project, shape (N, 3).

  • P_n (Tensor) – Normal vectors of the projection plane, shape (N, 3).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Projected vectors orthogonal to P_n, shape (N, 3).

static calculate_angles3(edge_vec, id3_reduce_ca, id3_expand_ba)[source]¶

Compute triplet angles for message passing.

Uses pre-computed PBC-correct edge vectors to avoid NaN for periodic self-image edges where pos[id_a] == pos[id_c].

Parameters:

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – PBC-aware edge vectors (id_c -> id_a), shape (nEdges, 3).

  • id3_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Edge index of c -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

  • id3_expand_ba (Tensor) – Edge index of b -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Triplet angles at atom a, shape (nTriplets,).

static calculate_angles(edge_vec, int_edge_vec, id4_expand_abd, id4_reduce_cab, id4_expand_intm_db, id4_reduce_intm_ca, id4_expand_intm_ab, id4_reduce_intm_ab)[source]¶

Compute the three angles required for quadruplet-based message passing.

Uses PBC-aware pre-computed edge vectors to avoid NaN on periodic self-image edges. The three returned angles correspond to:

  • angle_cab: angle at atom a between bonds c <- a -> b.

  • angle_abd: angle at atom b between bonds a <- b -> d.

  • angle_cabd: dihedral-like angle between the projected planes c <- a-b -> d.

Parameters:

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – Main-graph edge vectors (id_c -> id_a), shape (nEdges, 3).

  • int_edge_vec (Tensor) – Interaction-graph edge vectors (id4_int_b -> id4_int_a), shape (nIntEdges, 3).

  • id4_expand_abd (Tensor) – Quadruplet index mapping to the a-b-d triplet.

  • id4_reduce_cab (Tensor) – Quadruplet index mapping to the c-a-b triplet.

  • id4_expand_intm_db (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet index for the d -> b edge.

  • id4_reduce_intm_ca (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet index for the c -> a edge.

  • id4_expand_intm_ab (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet index for the a -> b edge.

  • id4_reduce_intm_ab (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet index for the a -> b edge (reduce side).

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

Returns:

Tuple (angle_cab, angle_abd, angle_cabd), each of shape (nQuadruplets,).

forward(z, edge_vec, edge_weight, id_a, id_c, id_swap, id3_expand_ba, id3_reduce_ca, Kidx3, int_edge_vec=None, int_edge_weight=None, Kidx4=None, id4_reduce_ca=None, id4_reduce_cab=None, id4_expand_abd=None, id4_reduce_intm_ca=None, id4_expand_intm_db=None, id4_reduce_intm_ab=None, id4_expand_intm_ab=None)[source]¶

Run the GemNet forward pass and return per-atom target predictions.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (nAtoms,).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – PBC-aware main-graph edge vectors (id_c -> id_a), shape (nEdges, 3).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Main-graph edge lengths (distances) in A, shape (nEdges,).

  • id_a (Tensor) – Target atom index for each edge (c -> a), shape (nEdges,).

  • id_c (Tensor) – Source atom index for each edge (c -> a), shape (nEdges,).

  • id_swap (Tensor) – Index mapping each edge to its reverse, shape (nEdges,).

  • id3_expand_ba (Tensor) – Edge index of b -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

  • id3_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Edge index of c -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

  • Kidx3 (Tensor) – Neighbor index within the sparse dense matrix for triplets, shape (nTriplets,).

  • int_edge_vec (Tensor | None) – Interaction-graph edge vectors, shape (nIntEdges, 3). Required when triplets_only=False.

  • int_edge_weight (Tensor | None) – Interaction-graph edge lengths in A, shape (nIntEdges,). Required when triplets_only=False.

  • Kidx4 (Tensor | None) – Neighbor index for the quadruplet sparse dense matrix. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_reduce_ca (Tensor | None) – Quadruplet edge index c -> a. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_reduce_cab (Tensor | None) – Quadruplet reduce index for c-a-b. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_expand_abd (Tensor | None) – Quadruplet expand index for a-b-d. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_reduce_intm_ca (Tensor | None) – Intermediate-triplet index c -> a. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_expand_intm_db (Tensor | None) – Intermediate-triplet index d -> b. Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_reduce_intm_ab (Tensor | None) – Intermediate-triplet index a -> b (reduce side). Required when triplets_only=False.

  • id4_expand_intm_ab (Tensor | None) – Intermediate-triplet index a -> b (expand side). Required when triplets_only=False.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom target predictions of shape (nAtoms, num_targets).

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize GemNet weights using its built-in He-orthogonal scheme.

GemNet uses its own weight initialization (He-orthogonal + variance- preserving scale factors), so the weights_init and bias_init arguments required by the base-class interface are intentionally ignored. A warning is emitted when called.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Ignored. Present for base-class interface compatibility.

  • bias_init (str) – Ignored. Present for base-class interface compatibility.

  • **kwargs – Additional keyword arguments (ignored).

Return type:

None

property signature: Config¶

Return the model signature as a Config.

Note

scale_file is always set to None in the signature. The fitted scale-factor values are carried by the model state_dict as non-trainable parameters, making saved checkpoints fully portable.
